datasets.name: billing_ai
queryLines:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', u.usage_date) AS month,
  CASE u.billing_origin_product
    WHEN 'MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'Model Serving'
    WHEN 'AI_FUNCTIONS' THEN 'AI Functions'
    WHEN 'AI_GATEWAY' THEN 'AI Gateway'
    WHEN 'AGENT_BRICKS' THEN 'Agent Bricks'
    WHEN 'AGENT_EVALUATION' THEN 'Agent Evaluation'
    WHEN 'SUPERVISOR_AGENT' THEN 'Supervisor Agent'
    ELSE u.billing_origin_product
  END AS billing_origin_product,
  CASE u.sku_name
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_ANTHROPIC_MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'Anthropic'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_OPENAI_MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'OpenAI'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_GEMINI_MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'Gemini'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_SERVERLESS_REAL_TIME_INFERENCE_US_EAST_N_VIRGINIA' THEN 'Serverless (US East)'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_SERVERLESS_REAL_TIME_INFERENCE_US_WEST_OREGON' THEN 'Serverless (US West)'
    ELSE u.sku_name
  END AS sku_name,
  CASE u.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE u.workspace_id
  END AS environment,
  SUM(u.usage_quantity) AS usage_quantity,
  ROUND(SUM(u.usage_quantity * COALESCE(lp.pricing.effective_list.default, 0)), 4) AS dollar_cost
FROM
  system.billing.usage u
    LEFT JOIN system.billing.list_prices lp
      ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
      AND u.usage_date >= CAST(lp.price_start_time AS DATE)
      AND (
        lp.price_end_time IS NULL
        OR u.usage_date < CAST(lp.price_end_time AS DATE)
      )
WHERE
  u.billing_origin_product IN (
    'AI_GATEWAY',
    'AI_FUNCTIONS',
    'MODEL_SERVING',
    'AGENT_BRICKS',
    'AGENT_EVALUATION',
    'SUPERVISOR_AGENT'
  )
  AND u.usage_date >= :date_range.min
  AND u.usage_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE u.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE u.workspace_id
      END
    )
  )
GROUP BY
  1,
  2,
  3,
  4
ORDER BY
  month

datasets.name: ai_gateway
queryLines:
SELECT
  ag.request_id,
  ag.event_time,
  ag.latency_ms,
  ag.destination_model,
  ag.input_tokens,
  ag.output_tokens,
  ag.total_tokens,
  ag.status_code,
  ag.requester,
  COALESCE(un.display_name, ag.requester) AS display_name,
  CASE ag.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE ag.workspace_id
  END AS environment
FROM
  system.ai_gateway.usage ag
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names un
      ON ag.requester = un.email
WHERE
  CAST(ag.event_time AS DATE) >= :date_range.min
  AND CAST(ag.event_time AS DATE) <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE ag.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE ag.workspace_id
      END
    )

columns.description: Percentage of requests with HTTP 200 status code
columns.expression: CONCAT(CAST(ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN `status_code` = 200 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS STRING), '%')

columns.description: Date extracted from event_time for daily aggregations
Count of non-200 status code requests
Percentage of requests with non-200 status
columns.expression: CONCAT(CAST(ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN `status_code` != 200 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS STRING), '%')

datasets.name: assistant_events
queryLines:
SELECT
  ae.event_id,
  ae.event_date,
  ae.initiated_by,
  COALESCE(un.display_name, ae.initiated_by) AS display_name,
  CASE ae.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE ae.workspace_id
  END AS environment
FROM
  system.access.assistant_events ae
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names un
      ON ae.initiated_by = un.email
WHERE
  ae.event_date >= :date_range.min
  AND ae.event_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE ae.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE ae.workspace_id
      END
    )

datasets.name: usage_patterns
queryLines:
SELECT
  DAYOFWEEK(event_time) AS day_of_week,
  DATE_FORMAT(event_time, 'EEE') AS day_name,
  HOUR(CONVERT_TIMEZONE('America/New_York', event_time)) AS hour_of_day_et,
  COUNT(*) AS request_count
FROM
  (
    SELECT
      event_time,
      workspace_id
    FROM
      system.access.assistant_events
    WHERE
      event_date >= :date_range.min
      AND event_date <= :date_range.max
      AND (
        COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
        OR array_contains(
          :env_filter,
          CASE workspace_id
            WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
            WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
            WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
            ELSE workspace_id
          END
        )
      )
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
      event_time,
      workspace_id
    FROM
      system.ai_gateway.usage
    WHERE
      CAST(event_time AS DATE) >= :date_range.min
      AND CAST(event_time AS DATE) <= :date_range.max
      AND (
        COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
        OR array_contains(
          :env_filter,
          CASE workspace_id
            WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
            WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
            WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
            ELSE workspace_id
          END
        )
      )
  ) combined
GROUP BY
  DAYOFWEEK(event_time),
  DATE_FORMAT(event_time, 'EEE'),
  HOUR(CONVERT_TIMEZONE('America/New_York', event_time))
ORDER BY
  day_of_week,
  hour_of_day_et

datasets.name: serving_endpoints
queryLines:
SELECT
  se.endpoint_name,
  se.served_entity_name,
  se.entity_type,
  se.entity_name,
  se.task,
  CONVERT_TIMEZONE('America/New_York', se.change_time) AS change_time_et,
  CASE se.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE se.workspace_id
  END AS environment
FROM
  system.serving.served_entities se
WHERE
  se.endpoint_delete_time IS NULL
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE se.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE se.workspace_id
      END
    )
  )
ORDER BY
  se.change_time DESC

columns.description: Percentage of endpoints that are agent-type
columns.expression: CONCAT(CAST(ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN `task` LIKE 'agent%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS STRING), '%')
SUM(CASE WHEN `task` LIKE 'agent%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)

datasets.name: genie_spaces
queryLines:
SELECT
  a.event_date,
  a.user_identity.email AS user_email,
  COALESCE(un.display_name, a.user_identity.email) AS display_name,
  a.action_name,
  a.request_params.space_id AS space_id,
  COALESCE(ln.display_name, a.request_params.space_id) AS space_name,
  a.request_params.conversation_id AS conversation_id,
  CASE a.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE a.workspace_id
  END AS environment
FROM
  system.access.audit a
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names un
      ON a.user_identity.email = un.email
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.genie_space_names ln
      ON a.request_params.space_id = ln.space_id
WHERE
  a.service_name = 'aibiGenie'
  AND a.action_name IN (
    'genieStartConversationMessage', 'createConversationMessage', 'createConversation'
  )
  AND a.event_date >= :date_range.min
  AND a.event_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE a.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE a.workspace_id
      END
    )

datasets.name: genie_spaces_top_users
queryLines:
SELECT
  COALESCE(un.display_name, a.user_identity.email) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(ln.display_name, a.request_params.space_id) AS space_name,
  COUNT(a.request_params.conversation_id) AS queries
FROM
  system.access.audit a
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names un
      ON a.user_identity.email = un.email
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.genie_space_names ln
      ON a.request_params.space_id = ln.space_id
WHERE
  a.service_name = 'aibiGenie'
  AND a.action_name IN (
    'genieStartConversationMessage', 'createConversationMessage', 'createConversation'
  )
  AND a.event_date >= :date_range.min
  AND a.event_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE a.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE a.workspace_id
      END
    )
  )
GROUP BY
  1,
  2
ORDER BY
  queries DESC
LIMIT 20

datasets.name: genie_spaces_daily
queryLines:
SELECT
  a.event_date,
  CASE a.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE a.workspace_id
  END AS environment,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.user_identity.email) AS unique_users
FROM
  system.access.audit a
WHERE
  a.service_name = 'aibiGenie'
  AND a.action_name IN (
    'genieStartConversationMessage', 'createConversationMessage', 'createConversation'
  )
  AND a.event_date >= :date_range.min
  AND a.event_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE a.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE a.workspace_id
      END
    )
  )
GROUP BY
  a.event_date,
  CASE a.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE a.workspace_id
  END
ORDER BY
  a.event_date

datasets.name: env_comparison
queryLines:
WITH assistant_metrics AS (
  SELECT
    CASE workspace_id
      WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
      WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
      WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    END AS environment,
    COUNT(*) AS genie_code_requests,
    COUNT(DISTINCT initiated_by) AS genie_code_users
  FROM
    system.access.assistant_events
  WHERE
    event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
  GROUP BY
    1
),
genie_spaces_metrics AS (
  SELECT
    CASE workspace_id
      WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
      WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
      WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    END AS environment,
    COUNT(*) AS genie_spaces_queries,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_identity.email) AS genie_spaces_users
  FROM
    system.access.audit
  WHERE
    service_name = 'aibiGenie'
    AND action_name IN (
      'genieStartConversationMessage', 'createConversationMessage', 'createConversation'
    )
    AND event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
  GROUP BY
    1
)
SELECT
  a.environment,
  a.genie_code_requests,
  a.genie_code_users,
  COALESCE(g.genie_spaces_queries, 0) AS genie_spaces_queries,
  COALESCE(g.genie_spaces_users, 0) AS genie_spaces_users
FROM
  assistant_metrics a
    LEFT JOIN genie_spaces_metrics g
      ON a.environment = g.environment
ORDER BY
  CASE a.environment
    WHEN 'DEV' THEN 1
    WHEN 'QA' THEN 2
    WHEN 'PROD' THEN 3
  END

datasets.name: assistant_events_top_users
queryLines:
SELECT
  COALESCE(un.display_name, ae.initiated_by) AS display_name,
  CASE ae.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE ae.workspace_id
  END AS environment,
  COUNT(*) AS submission_count
FROM
  system.access.assistant_events ae
    LEFT JOIN rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names un
      ON ae.initiated_by = un.email
WHERE
  ae.event_date >= :date_range.min
  AND ae.event_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE ae.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE ae.workspace_id
      END
    )
  )
GROUP BY
  1,
  2
ORDER BY
  submission_count DESC
LIMIT 20

datasets.name: growth_periods
queryLines:
WITH asst_raw AS ( -- Single scan: assistant_events 90d, tagged by period
  SELECT
    initiated_by,
    CASE
      WHEN event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS THEN 'current'
      WHEN event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 60 DAYS THEN 'prior'
      ELSE 'earlier'
    END AS period
  FROM
    system.access.assistant_events
  WHERE
    event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 90 DAYS
),
-- Single scan: ai_gateway 90d, tagged by period (may be empty)
gw_raw AS (
  SELECT
    requester,
    CASE
      WHEN CAST(event_time AS DATE) >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS THEN 'current'
      WHEN CAST(event_time AS DATE) >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 60 DAYS THEN 'prior'
      ELSE 'earlier'
    END AS period
  FROM
    system.ai_gateway.usage
  WHERE
    CAST(event_time AS DATE) >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 90 DAYS
),
-- Combined user IDs per period
all_users AS (
  SELECT
    initiated_by AS user_id,
    period
  FROM
    asst_raw
  UNION
  SELECT
    requester AS user_id,
    period
  FROM
    gw_raw
),
-- Aggregates per period
asst_agg AS (
  SELECT
    period,
    COUNT(*) AS requests
  FROM
    asst_raw
  GROUP BY
    period
),
gw_agg AS (
  SELECT
    period,
    COUNT(*) AS requests
  FROM
    gw_raw
  GROUP BY
    period
),
users_agg AS (
  SELECT
    period,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users
  FROM
    all_users
  WHERE
    user_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    period
),
-- Single scan: billing 60d, tagged by period with product-level splits
cost_agg AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN u.usage_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS THEN 'current'
      ELSE 'prior'
    END AS period,
    SUM(u.usage_quantity) AS total_dbus,
    ROUND(
      SUM(u.usage_quantity * COALESCE(lp.pricing.effective_list.default, 0)),
      2
    ) AS total_dollars,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN
          u.billing_origin_product IN ('AGENT_BRICKS', 'AGENT_EVALUATION', 'SUPERVISOR_AGENT')
        THEN
          u.usage_quantity
        ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS agent_dbus,
    ROUND(
      SUM(
        CASE
          WHEN
            u.billing_origin_product IN ('AGENT_BRICKS', 'AGENT_EVALUATION', 'SUPERVISOR_AGENT')
          THEN
            u.usage_quantity * COALESCE(lp.pricing.effective_list.default, 0)
          ELSE 0
        END
      ),
      2
    ) AS agent_dollars,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN u.billing_origin_product = 'MODEL_SERVING' THEN u.usage_quantity
        ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS serving_dbus,
    ROUND(
      SUM(
        CASE
          WHEN
            u.billing_origin_product = 'MODEL_SERVING'
          THEN
            u.usage_quantity * COALESCE(lp.pricing.effective_list.default, 0)
          ELSE 0
        END
      ),
      2
    ) AS serving_dollars
  FROM
    system.billing.usage u
      LEFT JOIN system.billing.list_prices lp
        ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
        AND u.usage_date >= CAST(lp.price_start_time AS DATE)
        AND (
          lp.price_end_time IS NULL
          OR u.usage_date < CAST(lp.price_end_time AS DATE)
        )
  WHERE
    u.billing_origin_product IN (
      'AI_GATEWAY',
      'AI_FUNCTIONS',
      'MODEL_SERVING',
      'AGENT_BRICKS',
      'AGENT_EVALUATION',
      'SUPERVISOR_AGENT'
    )
    AND u.usage_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 60 DAYS
  GROUP BY
    1
),
-- Retention: current-period users who were also active in prior period
current_retained AS (
  SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS retained_users
  FROM
    (
      SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id
      FROM
        all_users
      WHERE
        period = 'current'
        AND user_id IS NOT NULL
    ) c
      INNER JOIN (
        SELECT DISTINCT
          user_id
        FROM
          all_users
        WHERE
          period = 'prior'
          AND user_id IS NOT NULL
      ) p
        ON c.user_id = p.user_id
),
-- Retention: prior-period users who were also active in earlier period
prior_retained AS (
  SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS retained_users
  FROM
    (
      SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id
      FROM
        all_users
      WHERE
        period = 'prior'
        AND user_id IS NOT NULL
    ) c
      INNER JOIN (
        SELECT DISTINCT
          user_id
        FROM
          all_users
        WHERE
          period = 'earlier'
          AND user_id IS NOT NULL
      ) p
        ON c.user_id = p.user_id
),
-- New users: users in current NOT in prior (and prior NOT in earlier)
current_new AS (
  SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS new_users
  FROM
    (
      SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id
      FROM
        all_users
      WHERE
        period = 'current'
        AND user_id IS NOT NULL
    ) c
      LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT DISTINCT
          user_id
        FROM
          all_users
        WHERE
          period = 'prior'
          AND user_id IS NOT NULL
      ) p
        ON c.user_id = p.user_id
  WHERE
    p.user_id IS NULL
),
prior_new AS (
  SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS new_users
  FROM
    (
      SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id
      FROM
        all_users
      WHERE
        period = 'prior'
        AND user_id IS NOT NULL
    ) c
      LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT DISTINCT
          user_id
        FROM
          all_users
        WHERE
          period = 'earlier'
          AND user_id IS NOT NULL
      ) p
        ON c.user_id = p.user_id
  WHERE
    p.user_id IS NULL
),
-- Period anchor to guarantee rows even when some CTEs are empty
periods AS (
  SELECT
    'current' AS period
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    'prior' AS period
)
SELECT
  CASE p.period
    WHEN 'current' THEN DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE)
    ELSE DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS)
  END AS period_date,
  (COALESCE(ca.requests, 0) + COALESCE(cg.requests, 0)) AS total_requests,
  COALESCE(cu.unique_users, 0) AS unique_users,
  COALESCE(cc.total_dbus, 0) AS total_dbus,
  COALESCE(cc.total_dollars, 0) AS total_dollars,
  COALESCE(cc.agent_dbus, 0) AS agent_dbus,
  COALESCE(cc.agent_dollars, 0) AS agent_dollars,
  COALESCE(cc.serving_dbus, 0) AS serving_dbus,
  COALESCE(cc.serving_dollars, 0) AS serving_dollars,
  ROUND(COALESCE(cc.total_dbus, 0) / NULLIF(COALESCE(cu.unique_users, 0), 0), 1) AS dbus_per_user,
  ROUND(
    COALESCE(cc.total_dollars, 0) / NULLIF(COALESCE(cu.unique_users, 0), 0),
    2
  ) AS dollars_per_user,
  ROUND(
    (COALESCE(ca.requests, 0) + COALESCE(cg.requests, 0))
      * 1.0
      / NULLIF(COALESCE(cu.unique_users, 0), 0),
    1
  ) AS requests_per_user,
  ROUND(
    COALESCE(cc.total_dbus, 0) / NULLIF((COALESCE(ca.requests, 0) + COALESCE(cg.requests, 0)), 0),
    4
  ) AS dbus_per_request,
  CASE p.period
    WHEN
      'current'
    THEN
      ROUND(
        COALESCE(cr.retained_users, 0) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COALESCE(pu_prior.unique_users, 0), 0),
        1
      )
    ELSE
      ROUND(
        COALESCE(pr.retained_users, 0) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COALESCE(eu_earlier.unique_users, 0), 0),
        1
      )
  END AS retention_rate_pct,
  CASE p.period
    WHEN 'current' THEN COALESCE(cn.new_users, 0)
    ELSE COALESCE(pn.new_users, 0)
  END AS new_users
FROM
  periods p
    LEFT JOIN asst_agg ca
      ON ca.period = p.period
    LEFT JOIN gw_agg cg
      ON cg.period = p.period
    LEFT JOIN users_agg cu
      ON cu.period = p.period
    LEFT JOIN cost_agg cc
      ON cc.period = p.period
    LEFT JOIN current_retained cr
      ON p.period = 'current'
    LEFT JOIN prior_retained pr
      ON p.period = 'prior'
    LEFT JOIN current_new cn
      ON p.period = 'current'
    LEFT JOIN prior_new pn
      ON p.period = 'prior'
    LEFT JOIN users_agg pu_prior
      ON pu_prior.period = 'prior'
      AND p.period = 'current'
    LEFT JOIN users_agg eu_earlier
      ON eu_earlier.period = 'earlier'
      AND p.period = 'prior'
ORDER BY
  period_date DESC

datasets.name: cost_forecast
queryLines:
WITH monthly_cost AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', u.usage_date) AS ds,
    ROUND(SUM(u.usage_quantity * COALESCE(lp.pricing.effective_list.default, 0)), 2) AS total_cost
  FROM
    system.billing.usage u
      LEFT JOIN system.billing.list_prices lp
        ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
        AND u.usage_date >= CAST(lp.price_start_time AS DATE)
        AND (
          lp.price_end_time IS NULL
          OR u.usage_date < CAST(lp.price_end_time AS DATE)
        )
  WHERE
    u.billing_origin_product IN (
      'AI_GATEWAY',
      'AI_FUNCTIONS',
      'MODEL_SERVING',
      'AGENT_BRICKS',
      'AGENT_EVALUATION',
      'SUPERVISOR_AGENT'
    )
    AND u.usage_date < DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE)
  GROUP BY
    1
),
forecasted AS (
  SELECT
    *
  FROM
    ai_forecast(
      TABLE(monthly_cost),
      horizon => DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL 2 MONTHS,
      time_col => 'ds',
      value_col => 'total_cost',
      frequency => 'month',
      parameters => '{"global_floor": 0}'
    )
)
SELECT
  ds,
  total_cost AS actual,
  CAST(NULL AS DOUBLE) AS forecast,
  CAST(NULL AS DOUBLE) AS forecast_upper,
  CAST(NULL AS DOUBLE) AS forecast_lower
FROM
  monthly_cost
UNION ALL
SELECT
  ds,
  CAST(NULL AS DOUBLE),
  total_cost,
  total_cost,
  total_cost
FROM
  monthly_cost
WHERE
  ds
    = (
      SELECT
        MAX(ds)
      FROM
        monthly_cost
    )
UNION ALL
SELECT
  ds,
  CAST(NULL AS DOUBLE),
  total_cost_forecast AS forecast,
  total_cost_upper AS forecast_upper,
  total_cost_lower AS forecast_lower
FROM
  forecasted
ORDER BY
  ds

datasets.name: cost_by_sku
queryLines:
SELECT
  CASE u.sku_name
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_ANTHROPIC_MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'Anthropic'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_OPENAI_MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'OpenAI'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_GEMINI_MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'Gemini'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_SERVERLESS_REAL_TIME_INFERENCE_US_EAST_N_VIRGINIA' THEN 'Serverless (US East)'
    WHEN 'ENTERPRISE_SERVERLESS_REAL_TIME_INFERENCE_US_WEST_OREGON' THEN 'Serverless (US West)'
    ELSE u.sku_name
  END AS sku_name,
  CASE u.billing_origin_product
    WHEN 'MODEL_SERVING' THEN 'Model Serving'
    WHEN 'SUPERVISOR_AGENT' THEN 'Supervisor Agent'
    WHEN 'AI_FUNCTIONS' THEN 'AI Functions'
    WHEN 'AI_GATEWAY' THEN 'AI Gateway'
    WHEN 'AGENT_BRICKS' THEN 'Agent Bricks'
    WHEN 'AGENT_EVALUATION' THEN 'Agent Evaluation'
    ELSE u.billing_origin_product
  END AS billing_origin_product,
  CASE u.workspace_id
    WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
    WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
    WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
    ELSE u.workspace_id
  END AS environment,
  ROUND(SUM(u.usage_quantity), 2) AS total_dbus,
  ROUND(SUM(u.usage_quantity * COALESCE(lp.pricing.effective_list.default, 0)), 2) AS dollar_cost
FROM
  system.billing.usage u
    LEFT JOIN system.billing.list_prices lp
      ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
      AND u.usage_date >= CAST(lp.price_start_time AS DATE)
      AND (
        lp.price_end_time IS NULL
        OR u.usage_date < CAST(lp.price_end_time AS DATE)
      )
WHERE
  u.billing_origin_product IN (
    'AI_GATEWAY',
    'AI_FUNCTIONS',
    'MODEL_SERVING',
    'AGENT_BRICKS',
    'AGENT_EVALUATION',
    'SUPERVISOR_AGENT'
  )
  AND u.usage_date >= :date_range.min
  AND u.usage_date <= :date_range.max
  AND (
    COALESCE(array_size(:env_filter), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(
      :env_filter,
      CASE u.workspace_id
        WHEN '1160807094185734' THEN 'DEV'
        WHEN '748821712601607' THEN 'QA'
        WHEN '759839464639728' THEN 'PROD'
        ELSE u.workspace_id
      END
    )
  )
GROUP BY
  1,
  2,
  3
ORDER BY
  dollar_cost DESC

**Deep Dives:** [Genie Code Adoption](https://rai-qa.cloud.databricks.com/dashboardsv3/01f14a3cab8e1775858ba6c67419c3fa/published?o=748821712601607) · [Genie Spaces Adoption](https://rai-qa.cloud.databricks.com/dashboardsv3/01f14e0ddaa21d03983b7789a783d0b6/published?o=748821712601607)

lines: ### Serving Endpoints
Active model serving infrastructure across all environments.
## Glossary
Definitions for AI features, billing products, and data sources referenced in this dashboard.

### AI Features (What Users Interact With)

**Genie Code** — The in-editor AI assistant embedded in notebooks, SQL editor, and file editor. Generates, explains, and debugs code based on natural language prompts. Every submission (prompt → response) is one event.

*Use case: A data engineer types "write a query to deduplicate this table" and gets working SQL back instantly.*

---

**Genie Spaces (AI/BI)** — Conversational analytics rooms where business users ask questions in plain English against curated datasets. No code required — Genie translates questions into SQL and returns tables/charts.

*Use case: A finance analyst asks "What was Q1 revenue by region?" in a Genie Space connected to the sales fact table.*

---

**AI Gateway** — A centralized proxy layer for all LLM API calls. Provides rate limiting, cost tracking, guardrails, and routing across model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.). Once configured, all programmatic LLM calls route through it.

*Use case: A production application calls GPT-4 through AI Gateway, which enforces token limits, logs usage per user, and falls back to a secondary model if the primary is throttled.*

### Billing Products (How Cost is Categorized)

**Model Serving** (`MODEL_SERVING`) — Infrastructure cost for hosting and running ML/AI models on endpoints. Includes LLM inference (Foundation Model APIs), custom model serving, and feature serving. Dominates total AI spend (typically 90%+) because it covers every token processed by hosted models.

*Why it's separated on the Executive Summary: Its magnitude dwarfs other products, making their trends invisible in a shared chart.*

---

**AI Functions** (`AI_FUNCTIONS`) — DBUs consumed by SQL AI functions (`ai_query()`, `ai_generate()`, `ai_classify()`, `ai_extract()`, etc.) used directly in SQL queries and notebooks.

*Use case:* `SELECT ai_classify(comment, ARRAY('positive','negative')) FROM reviews` *— classifies sentiment at query time.*

---

**AI Gateway** (`AI_GATEWAY`) — DBUs for requests routed through AI Gateway endpoints (distinct from Model Serving, which covers the model compute itself).

*Use case: Gateway overhead for logging, guardrail evaluation, and routing decisions on each API call.*

---

**Agent Bricks** (`AGENT_BRICKS`) — Cost of running deployed agent endpoints built with frameworks like LangChain, LlamaIndex, or custom agent code hosted on Databricks Model Serving.

*Use case: A customer-support agent endpoint that retrieves knowledge base articles, reasons through the query, and generates a response.*

---

**Agent Evaluation** (`AGENT_EVALUATION`) — DBUs for evaluating agent quality using MLflow evaluation (correctness scoring, judge models, test harnesses).

*Use case: Running 500 test cases against an agent to measure retrieval accuracy and answer faithfulness before promoting to production.*

---

**Supervisor Agent** (`SUPERVISOR_AGENT`) — Cost of multi-agent orchestration where a supervisor agent coordinates multiple sub-agents to complete complex tasks.

*Use case: A "research assistant" supervisor that delegates to a retrieval agent, a summarization agent, and a citation-checking agent in sequence.*

### Environments

Three workspaces map to deployment stages. The global **Environment** filter slices all pages by these values.

**DEV** — Workspace `1160807094185734` — Development and experimentation

**QA** — Workspace `748821712601607` — Testing and validation

**PROD** — Workspace `759839464639728` — Production workloads

### Data Sources

All metrics are derived from Databricks system tables and two lookup tables maintained by a weekly enrichment job.

**`system.access.assistant_events`** — Every Genie Code prompt/response (in-editor AI assistant)

**`system.access.audit`** — All workspace actions — used here to extract Genie Spaces queries

**`system.billing.usage`** — DBU consumption by product, workspace, and date

**`system.billing.list_prices`** — SKU-level list pricing; joined to usage for dollar cost estimates

**`system.ai_gateway.usage`** — Per-request AI Gateway logs (tokens, latency, model, user)

**`system.serving.served_entities`** — Active model serving endpoint configurations

**`rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names`** — Materialized email → display name lookup (442 users). Eliminates repeated audit scans for user names. Refreshed weekly.

**`rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.genie_space_names`** — Space ID → title lookup via REST API. Resolves 11 QA spaces; DEV/PROD need tokens in `pltf` secret scope. Refreshed weekly (Mon 8 AM ET).

### Dashboard Notes

* **Model Serving** — Cost page shows Model Serving Cost ($) and Model Serving DBUs for endpoint hosting + inference. Executive Summary and Cost page stacked bar charts both exclude Model Serving because its magnitude (90%+ of spend) makes other products unreadable at the same scale

* **Model Serving SKU Breakdown** — Horizontal bar chart on Cost page showing dollar cost split by SKU (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Serverless US East, Serverless US West). Labels are aliased from raw ENTERPRISE_* SKU names via SQL CASE statements

* **Dollar-denominated charts** — All stacked bar charts (Monthly AI Products Cost, Monthly AI Cost by Product, Monthly Agent Cost) display USD rather than raw DBUs for clearer cost communication. Chart legends use friendly product names via SQL CASE in the billing_ai dataset

* **AI Cost Forecast** — Uses `ai_forecast()` (Databricks SQL AI function) to project total monthly AI cost 2 months forward with 95% confidence interval. Trains on complete historical months only (excludes partial current month). Rendered as a forecast-line chart with actual (solid) vs predicted (with shaded confidence band). Uses `global_floor: 0` to prevent negative projections

* **Monthly AI Cost History vs counters** — The history line chart shows ALL complete months since Jan 2024. The counters compare only the two most recent 30-day windows. This means the chart may show a decline from a peak (e.g., Jan 2026 spike) while the counter shows month-over-month growth — both are correct, different time horizons

* **Adoption by Environment** — Shows Genie Code requests/users and Genie Spaces queries/users side-by-side per workspace. Intentionally excludes DBU cost (which lives on the Cost page) to avoid implying a causal link between request volume and spend

* **Dollar amounts (Cost column)** — Calculated by joining `system.billing.usage` to `system.billing.list_prices` on SKU name and date window. Uses `pricing.effective_list.default` (resolves list vs promotional pricing). Current rate: $0.07/DBU for all AI SKUs. **Caveat:** These are list prices — contracted rates may differ

* **AI Gateway** — `system.ai_gateway.usage` is currently empty in this workspace (no AI Gateway routes configured). All Infrastructure page Gateway widgets (Success Rate, Input/Output Tokens, Top Models, Latency, Error Rate, Daily Volume) are pre-wired and will auto-populate once routes are created. The `growth_periods` dataset uses LEFT JOINs to remain resilient to empty gateway data

* **Date Range filter** — Defaults to 30 days rolling; the `env_comparison`, `growth_periods`, and `cost_forecast` datasets always use fixed windows regardless of filter

* **Environment filter** — Controls all datasets except `env_comparison`, `growth_periods`, and `cost_forecast` (which are fixed-window)

* **Top 20 users** — Adoption page tables show the 20 most active users (by submission/query count) within the selected date range and environment

* **All times** — Displayed in Eastern Time (ET) unless otherwise noted

* **Period arrows** — KPI counters show ↑/↓ percentage change vs the prior 30-day window. Applies to 14 counters: Total AI Cost ($), Total AI DBUs, Total AI Requests, Unique AI Users, Cost per User (DBUs), Cost per User ($), Cost per Request, Avg Requests/User, Retention Rate, New Users, Agent Cost ($), Model Serving Cost ($), Agent DBUs, and Model Serving DBUs

* **Conditional arrow colors** — Period arrows are color-coded by metric type. Green (↑) / red (↓) for engagement metrics (requests, users, retention, new users, avg requests/user). Red (↑) / green (↓) for cost metrics (DBUs, dollars, cost-per-request, cost-per-user) where increases represent higher spend

* **New Users metric** — Counts users active in the current 30-day period who were NOT active in the prior 30-day period (LEFT JOIN exclusion pattern)

* **Cost per User ($)** — Total dollar cost ÷ unique users for the 30-day window

* **Cost page section headers** — Dollar Cost and DBU Consumption sections clearly separate currency-denominated KPIs from raw DBU consumption counters

* **Friendly labels** — All raw system identifiers are aliased to readable names via SQL CASE statements in dataset queries: SKU names (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Serverless US East/West), product names (Model Serving, AI Functions, AI Gateway, Agent Bricks, Agent Evaluation, Supervisor Agent), and environments (DEV, QA, PROD)

* **Color consistency** — Charts with color-encoded dimensions use explicit color mappings for cross-chart consistency. Products: Model Serving (#1976D2 blue), AI Functions (#F57C00 orange), AI Gateway (#7B1FA2 purple), Agent Bricks (#388E3C green), Agent Evaluation (#D32F2F red), Supervisor Agent (#0097A7 teal). Environments: DEV (#1976D2 blue), QA (#F57C00 orange), PROD (#388E3C green). These mappings ensure the same category renders the same color regardless of which chart subset it appears in

* **Performance optimizations** — User display names resolved via `rai_qa_uc.ai_usage_monitor.user_display_names` lookup table (not audit CTE scans). Billing consolidated to one month-level dataset serving all Cost page widgets. `growth_periods` uses single-scan-per-source pattern (3 table scans total). Users inactive >90d show email addresses instead of display names.

### Version History

| Version | Date | Author | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1.2.0 | 2026-05-12 | Brandon Croom | Removed conditional color formatting from all 16 period counters (Executive Summary + Cost page). Counter `target.style.rules` evaluated against unsigned change magnitude — decreases never triggered red. Arrows (↑/↓) correctly indicate direction without misleading color signals. |
| 1.1.0 | 2026-05-12 | Brandon Croom | Cross-dashboard consistency standardization: environment colors updated from Material 700 to Material 500 (#2196F3/#FF9800/#4CAF50), counter red standardized to #F44336, comparison operators changed from > to >= for consistency with Genie Code and Genie Spaces dashboards. |
| 1.0.0 | 2026-05-12 | Brandon Croom | Initial versioned release — 5 content pages, 14 period counters, glossary. Includes: Executive Summary, Adoption, Cost (with ai_forecast projection), Infrastructure, Glossary. Datasets: 12 SQL queries against system tables + 2 lookup tables. Features: conditional arrow colors (green/red by metric type), friendly name aliasing via SQL CASE, cross-environment comparison, Model Serving SKU breakdown. |
